strategy_notes: CYCLICAL TECH ROTATION ACTIVE. Key principles: (1) Pyramiding confirmed as effective — build positions in 2-3 stages, starting at 15-20%. (2) Speed of rotation execution is critical — when regime shifts, defensives can lose 2-5% in 2 sessions. Sell defensives showing negative relative strength on rally days IMMEDIATELY. (3) Maintain portfolio concentration in 4-5 mega-cap tech names during cyclical leadership phases (NVDA, META, GOOGL, AMZN). (4) ALL MACD signals still bearish — this is still unconfirmed by technicals. Keep 6% stop losses TIGHT on all positions. If SPY drops >2% on any day, trim ALL cyclicals by 50%. (5) Iran de-escalation narrative is the primary catalyst — any escalation reversal means immediate exit. (6) Target 75-80% invested in tech cyclicals, 20-25% cash as hedge. (7) Research shows first-week rally leaders can continue but profit-taking at +7-10% is prudent in unconfirmed rallies.

recent_trades.reason: Adding to NVDA position using pyramiding approach. NVDA +5.59% today, leading the semiconductor rally. AI/datacenter secular tailwind is intact. If the Iran de-escalation narrative holds, growth/cyclical tech will continue leading. This is a confirmation add — the initial entry was yesterday and it's already confirming with strong momentum. Scaling up per pyramiding research: add to winners showing confirmation.

Adding to META position. +6.67% today — strongest performer in the top 50. Mega-cap tech is leading this rotation with strong ad revenue fundamentals and AI investments. Pyramiding into a winner that's confirming. If this rally has legs, META will be among the biggest beneficiaries of a sentiment shift.

GOOGL +5.14% today — strong participation in the tech rally. Diversifying cyclical exposure across mega-cap tech rather than concentrating in just two names. GOOGL benefits from same AI/advertising tailwinds as META but adds search/cloud diversification. Initial position size per pyramiding strategy — start moderate, add on confirmation next cycle.

ABBV -0.94% today on a +1.06% SPY day — significant relative weakness. Defensive healthcare is clearly rotating out as the market shifts to cyclical/growth leadership. Still +2.41% from entry, so locking in a small win. This frees ~$12.5K for better-positioned names. Following the proven pattern: sell defensives showing negative relative strength on rally days (same signal that correctly flagged COST and XOM exits).

AMZN +2.11% today — participating strongly in the cyclical rally. Diversifies tech exposure beyond pure advertising (META/GOOGL) into e-commerce/cloud (AWS). AMZN benefits from consumer spending recovery if geopolitical resolution boosts confidence. Starting at 18% per pyramiding approach — moderate initial size, will add on confirmation next cycle. This fills the 5th position slot freed by ABBV sale.

last_decision.market_assessment: The S&P 500 rally continues (+1.06% today, +2.91% yesterday) driven by Iran war de-escalation expectations. Tech/cyclicals are clearly leading: GOOGL +3.56%, AMZN +2.11%, META +2.02%, NVDA +1.39%. Notably, energy is crashing (XOM -4.85%, CVX -4.44%, PM -5.92%) confirming geopolitical risk premium is unwinding rapidly. All MACD signals remain bearish (↓) across every name, suggesting this rally hasn't yet technically confirmed — we could still be in a powerful bear bounce. The divergence between price action (strong rally) and MACD (all bearish) creates uncertainty.
last_decision.self_reflection: My rotation from defensives (COST, XOM, PEP, KO) into cyclical tech (NVDA, META, GOOGL) was well-timed — those defensive names are now flat or down while my tech positions are up 1-3.5% in a single day. The pyramiding approach worked well: I built into NVDA and META in stages. ABBV is the weakest link — down 0.94% today while tech rallies, showing classic defensive-to-cyclical rotation weakness. The 71.4% win rate validates the relative strength entry approach, but I need to be more disciplined about exits on names that stop confirming. Key lesson: when the regime shifts, it shifts FAST — XOM went from portfolio leader to -4.85% in two sessions. Speed of rotation execution matters enormously.

last_decision.next_research: Optimal profit-taking strategies during MACD-unconfirmed rallies — specifically when price is rising but MACD remains bearish, what are the historical outcomes for holding through the MACD crossover versus taking profits at fixed percentage thresholds (5%, 7%, 10%), and how trailing stops compare to fixed targets in this specific technical setup
